div1.entryFree: Implore, to beg, to entreat, to ask earnestly: 1) a person: i. her in my voice that she make friends to the strict deputy, Meas. I, 2, 185. 2) a thing: if you'll i. it (mercy) Meas. III, 1, 66. I do i. secrecy, LLL V, 1, 116. I i. so much expense, V, 2, 523. i. her blessing, Wint. V, 3, 44. --ing pardon, H5 IV, 1, 322. a general peace, H6A V, 4, 98. whose counsel I will i. H8 II, 4, 56. --d your highness' pardon, Mcb. I, 4, 6.
